Mobius
Adversarial Agent Swarm GitHub ↗
Open-source Python package

Agents compete.
Losers evolve.

$ pip install mobius-swarm
Status Active · Open Source · Python 3.12+

Mobius runs your task across Claude, Gemini, and GPT simultaneously. A cross-family judge panel picks the winner. The losers study the champion and rewrite themselves. Repeat until excellence compounds.

The premise

One model is never enough.

Every frontier model has blind spots that benchmarks never expose. Claude excels at nuance and reasoning. Gemini dominates breadth. GPT-4o surprises with lateral thinking. The honest answer to "which AI should I use?" is: all three, in competition, on your actual work.

Mobius operationalizes that insight. Instead of picking a favorite and committing, you run agents from each provider simultaneously against real tasks, score them on output quality, and let performance — not marketing benchmarks — determine who wins. The losers don't just lose. They study the winner's approach and rewrite their strategies. Every round, your swarm gets sharper.

This is not evaluation. This is evolution under pressure.

The loop

Four phases. Infinite pressure.

Every Mobius run executes the same adversarial cycle — simple in structure, relentless in compounding effect.

  1. 01

    Compete

    You give Mobius a task. It spins up agents from Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI simultaneously. Each attacks the problem with its own strategy, prompt style, and skill set. No handicaps. No favoritism.

  2. 02

    Judge

    A cross-family panel — Claude Opus, Gemini Pro, and GPT-4o — reviews every output. Shared-family bias is structurally eliminated. The panel scores quality, not provider loyalty. Dissent is recorded.

  3. 03

    Rank

    Results feed into an Elo system. Agents build reputations across tasks. The leaderboard surfaces which strategies consistently win — not once, but across domains, over hundreds of rounds.

  4. 04

    Evolve

    Losers receive structured critique: what the winner did better, what they missed, what to try next. They rewrite their approach. An agent that loses twenty competitions and evolves through each becomes formidable.

The competitors

Three families. One arena.

Each provider brings a distinct intelligence profile. Mobius forces them to compete on equal terms, on your problems, under real pressure.

Claude
Anthropic · Haiku / Sonnet / Opus

Deep reasoning, nuanced instruction-following, and careful judgment. Claude agents excel at extended chains of thought, structured output, and constitutional alignment. Default judge when using the free tier.

Gemini
Google · Flash / Pro

Breadth, grounding, and speed. Gemini agents bring Google-scale knowledge and fast throughput. Particularly strong at synthesis across large context windows and factual grounding with live data.

GPT
OpenAI · 4o / 4o-mini

Lateral creativity and tool-use mastery. GPT-4o agents consistently surprise with unconventional approaches to well-defined problems. Strong code generation and function-calling round out the swarm.

Rating system

Performance, not promises.

Every competition updates agent Elo scores. Consistent winners climb. Underperformers get targeted for evolution. The leaderboard is a living record of real-task performance, not marketing benchmarks.

1
Sonnet-Strategist
Anthropic · Claude
32 wins 1847
2
Flash-Lateral
Google · Gemini
28 wins 1791
3
4o-Pragmatist
OpenAI · GPT
21 wins 1734
The judge panel

  • Claude Opus — scores on reasoning depth, instruction adherence, and nuance. Available free via Claude Code Pro.

  • Gemini Pro — scores on factual breadth, synthesis quality, and structural clarity.

  • GPT-4o — scores on creativity, practical utility, and unconventional approaches.

Judges evaluate outputs from providers they don't belong to, eliminating same-family preference bias. Scores are averaged across the panel.

"

The question isn't which model is best. The question is which approach survives your problem under real pressure. Only the arena knows.

The Mobius design principle

Architecture

Six ideas that make it work.

  • 01
    Multi-provider swarm

    Agents are first-class citizens of their provider's ecosystem. Claude agents use Anthropic's tool-use natively. Gemini agents use Google's grounding. No least-common-denominator abstraction layer.

  • 02
    Elo rating system

    Borrowed from chess, adapted for AI. Each competition updates ratings based on expected vs. actual performance. A surprising upset from an underdog counts more than a favorite beating a weaker opponent.

  • 03
    Cross-family judge panel

    A Claude judge will favor Claude's style. So Mobius uses all three families. Structural diversity prevents any one model's preferences from dominating. The panel must converge, or scores cancel out.

  • 04
    Evolution loop

    Losers receive structured critique: what the winner did better, what they missed, what to attempt next. They update system prompts and strategies accordingly. Failure is signal — not waste.

  • 05
    Skills as weapons

    Agents don't just compete on prompts. They compete with different capabilities — web search, code execution, vector memory, specialized tools. Skill configuration is itself a competitive strategy.

  • 06
    Free-tier intelligence

    Opus-quality judging and agent creation runs through your existing Claude Pro subscription. No API fees for the expensive operations. Only the competing swarm calls the API, using fast cheap models like Haiku and Flash.

The interface

Built for the terminal.

Mobius is a Python CLI. Install it, initialize the database, and your first competition is three keystrokes away. No GUI, no configuration wizard, no cloud account required.

$ pip install -e ".[dev]"
$ mobius init Initialize the SQLite registry
$ mobius bootstrap Seed your first agent swarm
$ mobius run "your task" Run a full competition
$ mobius leaderboard View current Elo rankings
$ mobius evolve <spec> Refine underperformers
$ mobius loop --rounds 10 Self-improvement loop
Why it matters

Evolution, not selection.

Most teams pick one AI provider and commit. Integration has friction, benchmarks feel authoritative, and the switching cost seems high. But every frontier model has blind spots that benchmarks don't expose — and your tasks are not benchmark tasks.

Mobius shifts the question from "which model is best?" to "which approach wins on my problem?" That's a question you can answer empirically, not philosophically. Under real conditions. Against real tasks.

And then it goes further. The evolution loop means your agents don't just compete — they improve. An agent that loses twenty competitions and evolves through each loss becomes more precisely calibrated to your domain than any out-of-the-box model could be.

That's the Mobius insight: you don't need to pick the best model. You need a system that finds and refines the best approach, across all models, on your actual work. Pressure creates performance.

Providers in the arena 3

Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI agents compete simultaneously

Judge panel size 3×

Cross-family scoring eliminates same-provider preference bias

API cost for judging $0

Opus-quality judges via your existing Claude Code Pro subscription

Economics

Smart about cost, not just quality.

Mobius exploits a structural advantage: your Claude Pro subscription includes Opus-quality intelligence via Claude Code. The expensive, high-judgment operations — agent creation, judging, evolution strategy — run free. Only the competing swarm calls the API, using fast cheap models like Haiku and Flash.

A full bootstrap of your first agent team costs roughly $1.50. A ten-round self-improvement loop on a focused task runs under $5. You're not paying for intelligence — you already have it. You're paying for throughput.

Agent creation (Opus) Free
Competition judging (Opus) Free
Evolution strategy (Opus) Free
Swarm execution (Haiku/Flash) ~$0.15 / run
Full bootstrap (~10 agents) ~$1.50
10-round improvement loop < $5.00
Mobius

Adversarial agent swarm orchestrator with multi-provider support. Built with Python 3.12+, SQLite + sqlite-vec, and the claude-agent-sdk.

github.com/AaronGoldsmith/mobius ↗
Python 3.12+ SQLite + sqlite-vec claude-agent-sdk Anthropic · Google · OpenAI WCAG AA accessible
